name: Education First Credit Union
description_short: Education First Credit Union is a member-owned financial cooperative celebrating 90 years of service, offering savings, checking, loans, and financial education primarily to the educational community.
description_long:
Education First Credit Union has been serving its members for 90 years with a mission centered on enhancing financial positions through education, quality service, and value-minded products. The organization is driven by the conviction that people should plan for their financial futures and that personal relationships are essential to providing members with the education necessary to navigate financial decisions. While they serve the broader community, the credit union maintains a primary focus on serving members of the educational community, reflecting their heritage and appreciation for education's social impact.

The credit union offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including savings accounts for both short-term and long-term goals, multiple checking account options tailored to different activity levels and average balances, and competitive lending products. Their current loan offerings include auto loans starting at 4.99% APR, personal loans at 9.99% APR, home equity loans, home improvement loans ($1,000-$15,000 for up to 84 months at 9.49% APR), and private student loans through their CU Student Choice program. Members also benefit from access to exclusive savings through partner discounts, with members collectively saving over $2 billion through these partnerships.

Education First distinguishes itself through its commitment to financial education and member accessibility. The credit union provides free financial education resources including calculators, coaches, articles, and activities for all ages. They emphasize understanding member needs and making services accessible to support their lifestyle requirements. The organization actively engages members through community events (including their 90th Anniversary celebration with monthly social media contests) and maintains educational content through their Smart Money Financial Blog covering topics like credit scores, tax planning, and expense management.

The credit union appears well-established and member-focused with competitive rates and a genuine commitment to financial literacy. However, potential members should note that credit unions typically have membership requirements or field-of-membership restrictions that may limit accessibility compared to traditional banks. The website does not provide comprehensive details about all account fees, membership eligibility criteria, or the full scope of available products, which would require direct inquiry to fully evaluate.

pricing.guarantee_details: Contact provider for current pricing and guarantee details.

pros: Competitive auto loan rates as low as 4.99% APR, recently reduced
Comprehensive financial education resources including free calculators, coaches, articles, and activities for all ages
Members access exclusive partner discounts with reported $2 billion in collective savings
Multiple account options tailored to different activity levels and average balances
Home equity loans available with flexible terms (up to 84 months on home improvement loans)
Private student loans through CU Student Choice program for higher education financing
$25 referral bonus for bringing new members into the credit union
90-year track record and active community engagement with anniversary celebrations and events
cons: Credit union membership typically requires meeting eligibility criteria or field-of-membership restrictions not fully detailed on website
Website does not clearly display account fees, minimum balances, or complete fee schedules
Limited information about business loan products despite mention in mission statement
No mention of mobile app features, online banking capabilities, or digital service details
Personal loan rates start at 9.99% APR, which is higher than some alternative lenders
best_for: Education sector employees and students seeking member-owned financial services with educational focus
Consumers prioritizing financial education and seeking guidance beyond basic banking services
Individuals interested in auto financing with competitive rates and strong personal relationship banking
Members of the educational community eligible for field-of-membership requirements
diagnosis: Education First Credit Union is best for members of the educational community or those eligible for membership who value a member-owned institution with genuine commitment to financial education and competitive lending rates. The primary caveat is determining eligibility—credit unions typically have field-of-membership requirements that restrict access, so potential members should verify they qualify before applying.
services: Savings accounts for short-term and long-term goals
Multiple checking account options based on activity level and balance
Home improvement loans ($1,000-$15,000 for up to 84 months)
Private student loans through CU Student Choice program
Financial education resources (calculators, coaches, articles, activities)
Smart Money Financial Blog and educational content
typical_results_timeline: Results vary by individual situation. Contact the provider to discuss expected timelines for your specific needs.
